title: Fix RBAC: Principal-based admin authorization for user management

requirements.summary: Update frontend API calls to use authenticated actor for user management operations
acceptanceCriteria: All user management mutations (create, edit, delete user) in useQueries.ts use the authenticated actor from useActor hook.
The actor passed to SettingsPage and user management dialogs is the authenticated actor tied to the current Internet Identity session.
After signing in as admin, submitting the Create User form succeeds without an 'Unauthorized' error.
The actor is not the anonymous actor when admin-protected backend calls are made.

file_operations.description: Review and ensure all user management mutations (useCreateUser, useUpdateUser, useDeleteUser) are correctly using the authenticated actor from useActor. Verify that the actor dependency is properly set up so that these mutations only execute when an authenticated actor is available. Ensure no anonymous actor is used for admin-protected operations.

Verify that SettingsPage uses the authenticated actor from useActor hook when calling user management functions. Ensure all user CRUD operations (create, edit, delete) pass through mutations that use the authenticated actor. Add error handling to display authorization errors with clear user feedback if the backend returns 'Unauthorized' responses.

Ensure the CreateUserDialog component uses the useCreateUser mutation that depends on the authenticated actor. Verify that the mutation callback correctly handles backend authorization errors and displays them to the user via toast notifications. Confirm the actor is authenticated before allowing form submission.

Ensure the EditUserDialog component uses the useUpdateUser mutation that depends on the authenticated actor. Verify that the mutation callback correctly handles backend authorization errors and displays them to the user. Confirm the authenticated actor is being used for the update operation.

requirements.summary: Add UI for admins to grant or revoke admin role for other users
acceptanceCriteria: SettingsPage displays role management controls for existing users visible only to admins.
Granting or revoking admin role is reflected immediately in the user list.

file_operations.description: Add two new mutation hooks: useGrantAdminRole(userPrincipal: Principal) and useRevokeAdminRole(userPrincipal: Principal) that call the corresponding backend functions. Both mutations should use the authenticated actor from useActor. On success, invalidate the 'users' query key to refresh the user list. Use the authorization component's usage instructions as a reference for principal-based operations.

Add role management UI controls (e.g., Grant Admin / Revoke Admin buttons or dropdowns) next to each user in the user management table. Show these controls only when the current user is an admin (using the role verification from REQ-4). Wire the controls to call the useGrantAdminRole and useRevokeAdminRole mutations. Display toast notifications on success or error. Ensure the user list updates immediately after role changes via query invalidation.

requirements.summary: Display principal-verified admin role and control access to admin-only UI
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend calls getMyRole() after login to verify the authenticated user's actual role from the backend.
The admin role badge in the Header reflects the principal-verified role.
Admin-only navigation items (e.g., Settings in Sidebar) are shown/hidden based on the principal-verified role.

file_operations.description: Add a query hook useGetCallerRole() that calls the backend's getCallerRole() function to retrieve the authenticated user's role based on their Internet Identity principal. Use the authenticated actor from useActor. Enable the query only when an authenticated actor is available. Return the UserRole enum value. This query should be called after login to verify the user's actual backend role.

Use the useGetCallerRole query hook to fetch the principal-verified role from the backend. Display the admin role badge only when the backend confirms the user has admin role. Replace any locally stored or session-based role checks with the principal-verified role from the backend query. Show a loading state while the role is being fetched.

Use the useGetCallerRole query hook to determine whether to show the admin-only Settings navigation item. Show the Settings link only when the backend-verified role is 'admin'. Remove any local session-based role checks and rely solely on the principal-verified role from the backend. Handle loading state gracefully to avoid flickering the Settings item.

Update the AuthContext to trigger a call to getCallerRole() immediately after successful login, storing the principal-verified role in context state. Provide the backend-verified role via context so components can access it without redundant queries. Clear the role when the user logs out. This ensures the role is always synced with the backend's principal-based authorization.
